message: The detective must have gone to her place too.
he says things are back to normal, those detectives must be mentally drained.
Now that Guy is gone, I'm the only one who can comfort her.
Even after ringing the bell, there was no response. I thought she might be out, but then I realized the door wasn't locked.
The room faintly smelled of alcohol.
She stood there, holding a fruit knife in her hand.
She was just about to press the knife's blade against her slender wrist.
Did those detectives make it turn out like this?
It's okay, no one is going to interfere with you. So, put down that knife.
No, I don't want to, noooo!
Don't interfere, I can't take it anymore.
The moment the knife's tip cut my hand, I felt heat more than pain.
After that, the flowing blood and thumping pain attacked me.
Seeing the blood flowing from my hand, did I come back to my senses?
threw the knife and collapsed on the floor.
Blood drips down from my body.
I see her just crying and crying.
I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry.
No, it's okay. Don't worry about it; everyone has times like this.
I visited, but she seems to be out.
